Writing

Behavioural science
meets brand thinking.

An independent LinkedIn content series applying consumer psychology and behavioural economics to business, behaviour, culture, and everyday decision-making. Written for practitioners, not academia.

Behavioural UX

The Zeigarnik Effect and why unfinished things own your attention

How the brain treats incomplete tasks differently, and what every UX designer and brand strategist should understand about the loop that never closes.

Read on LinkedIn → Brand Psychology

Soft power, cultural mimicry, and how brands borrow belonging

Why the most effective brand strategies don't sell products. They sell identity. How behavioural mimicry underpins the most culturally resonant campaigns.

Read on LinkedIn → Consumer Behaviour

The psychology behind the choices we defend the most

Most decisions aren't rational, they're protective. Cognitive dissonance, post-purchase rationalisation, and what consumer loyalty is really made of.

Read on LinkedIn → Cognitive Bias

When you see a key in your dream, use it

Effort justification and why people consistently undervalue opportunities that arrive without friction, and what that means for how brands position ease.

Read on LinkedIn → Social Psychology

The most dangerous race you'll ever run is someone else's

Social comparison, aspirational positioning, and why benchmarking yourself against the wrong reference class is the fastest way to lose direction entirely.

Read on LinkedIn → Research + Strategy

What hospitals taught me about behaviour that no textbook did

Observations from experience in clinical and research settings. The gap between what people say and what they do is where the most useful insight lives.

Read on LinkedIn →

Visual Art

Digital composites
& paintings.

A personal body of work in fine arts, digital and collage art, and surreal composites. 8 years of formal fine arts training at MAFAQ, Delhi. Work exhibited across multiple exhibitions. Click any image to view full size.

About

Psychologist by training.
Strategist by instinct.

I hold an M.Sc. in Psychology (Merit, BPS-accredited) from the University of Dundee and a B.B.A. in Marketing & Psychology (Distinction) from IILM University — a combination that has always sat at the intersection of how people think, behave, and decide, and how those insights translate into real-world business and commercial contexts.

My research background includes co-leading a national study (N=1,800+) with India's Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, which gave me a rigorous understanding of behavioural data at scale. My creative background includes 8 years of formal fine arts training and work exhibited across multiple group exhibitions. In parallel, my experience working one-on-one with clients has given me a grounded understanding of individual behaviour — how people think, what drives their decisions, and how motivations play out in real-life contexts.

I am currently based in Delhi, open to opportunities worldwide in creative strategy, creative direction, brand communication, and content production.
